user.title: Connect to your Hue bridge
user.description: Enter the IP address of the Philips Hue bridge that owns your entertainment areas.

link.title: Pair with the bridge
link.description: Press the round link button on top of your Hue bridge, then select Submit within 30 seconds.

select_areas.title: Choose entertainment areas
select_areas.description: Select the entertainment areas you want to control. Manage the lights inside each area in the Hue app.

error.link_button: The link button was not pressed. Press it on the bridge and try again.
error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the bridge. Check the IP address and try again.

abort.no_areas: No entertainment areas were found on this bridge. Create one in the Hue app first.

init.title: Settings
init.description: Enable areas and tune the optional audio sources. Any Music Assistant player works out of the box; the Snapcast tap and the OpenSubsonic/Navidrome library link are optional (experimental/legacy) extras for setups that use them.

data.restore_lights: Restore exact light state when sync stops

activate.name: Activate music sync
activate.description: Start music sync for one or more entertainment areas.

auto_levels.name: Auto rungs
auto_levels.description: With mode auto, the intensities the picker may choose from. It climbs into Intense/Extreme only if included and the moment earns it.

auto_timing.name: Auto timing
auto_timing.description: Auto-calibrate the light delay per song (cancels the variable startup-hang lag) instead of using the manual timing offset.

effect.name: Effect
effect.description: Renderer/effect style: music, movies or fireworks.

colour.name: Colour
colour.description: Colour theme: album_art or a preset palette.

media_player.name: Media player
media_player.description: Media player to follow. Leave unset to auto-pick the one playing.

deactivate.name: Deactivate music sync
deactivate.description: Stop music sync for one or more entertainment areas.

set_options.name: Set music sync options
set_options.description: Change the mode or followed player live without restarting sync.

auto_levels.name: Auto rungs
auto_levels.description: With mode auto, the intensities the picker may choose from. It climbs into Intense/Extreme only if included and the moment earns it.

auto_timing.name: Auto timing
auto_timing.description: Auto-calibrate the light delay per song (cancels the variable startup-hang lag) instead of using the manual timing offset.

effect.name: Effect
effect.description: Renderer/effect style: music, movies or fireworks.

colour.name: Colour
colour.description: Colour theme: album_art or a preset palette.

prewarm_library.name: Pre-analyse music library
prewarm_library.description: Analyse your whole Music Assistant library in the background and cache the results to disk, so every track plays instantly with full offline track-map reaction the first time too. Gentle, resumable and incremental (a re-run picks up newly added tracks). For a Navidrome / OpenSubsonic library, set the library URL and login in the integration options first.

retry_failed.name: Retry failed tracks
retry_failed.description: Clear the recorded per-track failure verdicts (and ambient-only maps) first, so every previously failed track is analysed again.

force.name: Full re-analysis
force.description: Wipe the whole cache first and re-analyse every track from scratch, even up-to-date ones — to upgrade the entire library to a newer analysis format.

clear_library_cache.name: Delete library cache
clear_library_cache.description: Delete the whole on-disk track-map cache (every analysed song). Frees the disk space; tracks re-analyse on their next play, or run "Pre-analyse music library" to rebuild it.

analyze_track.name: Analyse one track
analyze_track.description: Diagnose (and re-analyse) a single song right away and post the full analysis verdict as a notification. Give a direct stream URL, an artist + title to look up in the Music Assistant library, or a media player that is currently playing.

url.name: Stream URL
url.description: Direct URL of the audio to analyse (anything ffmpeg can open).

artist.name: Artist
artist.description: Artist name, matched against the Music Assistant library.

title.name: Title
title.description: Track title, matched against the Music Assistant library.

media_player.name: Media player
media_player.description: Analyse whatever this player is playing right now.

force.name: Force re-analysis
force.description: Clear any cached map / failure verdict for the track and analyse it fresh (default on).
